description_long: This is a single, ripe red pear with a bulbous lower body tapering to a slender neck and a short, brown stem. The skin is predominantly a deep burgundy red with subtle speckles and a matte finish, showing some natural surface blemishes and a slight yellowish streak on one side. The pear's surface appears slightly rough and semi-soft, typical of fresh pears. The stem is woody and slightly curved, blending from brown to a lighter tan near the base. The overall shape is asymmetrical, with one side slightly more rounded and the other showing a gentle flattening. The fruit looks fresh and ready to eat, with no major bruises or signs of decay.
description: A ripe, deep red pear with a matte, slightly rough skin and a short brown stem. The surface shows natural blemishes and a yellowish streak, indicating freshness.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pear's broad, rounded base and the full length of the stem, with a smooth, even red surface.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the pear's asymmetrical shape, with a gentle curve and a subtle flattening on one side.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a prominent yellowish streak and more visible blemishes on the red skin, with the stem slightly off-center.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays a smooth, rounded contour with a consistent red color and minor surface speckles.
attribute_contextual_placement: stacked with other pears in a crate or displayed in a fruit basket
attribute_condition: fresh and ripe with minor natural blemishes
